heading: Payments

L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246section 8701 of Title 7

children.text: with respect to any cost-sharing payment obligation incurred by the Secretary, as soon as practicable after the obligation is incurred; and

children.label: A
children.text: as soon as practicable after October 1 of each calendar year; or

children.label: B
children.text: at the option of the Secretary, at any time prior to such date during the year that the obligation is incurred.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In making cost sharing payments to an owner or operator under a contract entered into under this subpart, the Secretary shall pay 50 percent of the cost of establishing water quality and conservation measures and practices required under each contract for which the Secretary determines that cost sharing is appropriate and in the public interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that cost sharing payments to an owner or operator under this subpart, when combined with the sum of payments from all other funding sources for measures and practices described in paragraph (1), do not exceed 100 percent of the total actual cost of establishing those measures and practices, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Mid-contract management grazing
children.label: B Mid-contract management grazing
children.text: The Secretary may not make any cost sharing payment to an owner or operator under this subpart pursuant to .section 3832(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Seed cost
children.label: C Seed cost
children.text: In the case of seed costs related to the establishment of cover, cost sharing payments under this subpart shall not exceed 50 percent of the actual cost of the seed mixture, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Other Federal cost share assistance
children.label: 3 Other Federal cost share assistance
children.text: Except in the case of incentive payments that are related to the cost of the establishment of a practice and received from eligible partners under the conservation reserve enhancement program under , an owner or operator shall not be eligible to receive or retain cost share assistance under this subsection if the owner or operator receives any other Federal cost share assistance with respect to the land under any other provision of law.section 3831a of this title

children.heading: Practice incentives for continuous practices
children.label: 4 Practice incentives for continuous practices
children.text: In addition to the cost sharing payment described in this subsection, the Secretary shall make an incentive payment to an owner or operator of land enrolled under in an amount not to exceed 50 percent of the actual cost of establishing all measures and practices described in paragraph (1), including seed costs related to the establishment of cover, as determined by the Secretary.section 3831(d)(6) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Using funds made available under , the Secretary may make incentive payments to an owner or operator of eligible land in an amount sufficient to encourage proper thinning and other practices to improve the condition of resources, promote forest management, or enhance wildlife habitat on the land.section 3841(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: A payment described in paragraph (1) may not exceed 100 percent of the total cost of thinning and other practices conducted by the owner or operator.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount necessary to encourage owners or operators of highly erodible cropland or other eligible lands to participate in the program established by this subpart;

children.label: B
children.text: the impact on the local farmland rental market; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amounts payable to owners or operators in the form of rental payments under contracts entered into under this subpart may be determined through the submission of applications for such contracts by owners and operators in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), if land subject to a contract entered into under this subpart is reenrolled under or has been previously enrolled in the conservation reserve, the annual rental payment shall be in an amount that is not more than 85 percent in the case of general enrollment contacts, or 90 percent in the case of continuous enrollment contracts, of the applicable estimated average county rental rate published pursuant to paragraph (4) for the year in which the reenrollment occurs.section 3831(h)(1) of this title

children.heading: Conservation reserve enhancement program
children.label: ii Conservation reserve enhancement program
children.text: The reduction in annual rental payments under clause (i) may be waived as part of the negotiation between the Secretary and an eligible partner to enter into a conservation reserve enhancement program agreement under .section 3831a of this title

children.heading: Grasslands
children.label: C Grasslands
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), in the case of eligible land described in , the Secretary shall make annual payments in an amount that is not more than 75 percent of the grazing value of the land covered by the contract.section 3831(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Continuous sign-up incentives
children.label: D Continuous sign-up incentives
children.text: The Secretary shall make an incentive payment to the owner or operator of land enrolled under at the time of initial enrollment in an amount equal to 32.5 percent of the amount of the first annual rental payment under subparagraph (A).section 3831(d)(6) of this title

children.heading: Evaluation of offers
children.label: A Evaluation of offers
children.text: In determining the acceptability of contract offers, the Secretary may take into consideration the extent to which enrollment of the land that is the subject of the contract offer would improve soil resources, water quality, or wildlife habitat or provide other environmental benefits.

children.heading: Establishment of different criteria in various States and regions
children.label: B Establishment of different criteria in various States and regions
children.text: The Secretary may establish different criteria for determining the acceptability of contract offers in various States and regions of the United States based on the extent to which water quality or wildlife habitat may be improved or erosion may be abated.

children.heading: Local preference
children.label: C Local preference
children.text: In determining the acceptability of contract offers for new enrollments, the Secretary shall accept, to the maximum extent practicable, an offer from an owner or operator that is a resident of the county in which the land is located or of a contiguous county if, as determined by the Secretary, the land would provide at least equivalent conservation benefits to land under competing offers.

children.heading: Annual estimates
children.label: A Annual estimates
children.text: The Secretary (acting through the National Agricultural Statistics Service) shall annually conduct a survey of per acre estimates of county average market dryland and irrigated cash rental rates for cropland and pastureland in all counties or equivalent subdivisions within each State that have 20,000 acres or more of cropland and pastureland, and shall publish the estimates derived from the survey not later than September 15 of each year.

children.heading: Public availability of estimates
children.label: B Public availability of estimates
children.text: The estimates derived from the survey conducted under subparagraph (A) and the average current and previous soil rental rates for each county shall be maintained on a website of the Department of Agriculture for use by the general public.

children.heading: Use
children.label: C Use
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the estimates derived from the survey conducted under subparagraph (A) relating to dryland cash rental rates as a factor in determining rental rates under this section in a manner determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Submission of additional information by State FSA offices and CREP partners
children.label: D Submission of additional information by State FSA offices and CREP partners

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide an opportunity for State Committees of the Farm Service Agency or eligible partners (as defined in ) in conservation reserve enhancement programs under to propose an alternative soil rental rate prior to finalizing new rates, on the condition that documentation described in clause (ii) is provided to support the proposed alternative.section 3831a(a) of this titlesection 3831a of this title

children.label: I
children.text: an average of cash rents from a random sample of lease agreements;

children.label: II
children.text: cash rent estimates from a published survey;

children.label: III
children.text: neighboring county estimate comparisons from the National Agricultural Statistics Service;

children.label: IV
children.text: an average of cash rents from Farm Service Agency farm business plans;

children.label: V
children.text: models that estimate cash rents, such as models that use returns to estimate crop production or land value data; or

children.label: VI
children.text: other documentation, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: iii Notification
children.text: Not less than 14 days prior to the announcement of new or revised soil rental rates, the Secretary shall offer a briefing to the Chairman and Ranking Member of the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Chairman and Ranking Member of the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate, including information on and the rationale for the alternative rates proposed under clause (i) that were accepted or rejected.

children.label: i
children.text: 85 percent of the estimated rental rate determined under this paragraph for general enrollment; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 90 percent of the estimated rental rate determined under this paragraph for continuous enrollment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, payments under this subpart shall be made in cash in such amount and on such time schedule as is agreed on and specified in the contract.

children.heading: Advance payment
children.label: 2 Advance payment
children.text: Payments under this subpart may be made in advance of determination of performance.

children.heading: Payments on death, disability, or succession
children.label: f Payments on death, disability, or succession
children.text: If an owner or operator that is entitled to a payment under a contract entered into under this subpart dies, becomes incompetent, is otherwise unable to receive the payment, or is succeeded by another person that renders or completes the required performance, the Secretary shall make the payment, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary and without regard to any other provision of law, in such manner as the Secretary determines is fair and reasonable in light of all of the circumstances.

children.heading: Payment limitation for rental payments
children.label: g Payment limitation for rental payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the total amount of rental payments received by a person or legal entity, directly or indirectly, under this subpart for any fiscal year may not exceed $50,000.

children.heading: Wellhead protection
children.label: 2 Wellhead protection
children.text: Paragraph (1) and shall not apply to rental payments received by a rural water district or association for land that is enrolled under this subpart for the purpose of protecting a wellhead.section 1308–3a(b) of title 7

children.heading: Other State or local assistance
children.label: h Other State or local assistance
children.text: In addition to any payment under this subpart, an owner or operator may receive cost share assistance, rental payments, or tax benefits from a State or subdivision thereof for enrolling land in the conservation reserve program.
